channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Bringing hope and joy: superheroes for children with cancer in Kosovo
item.description: As the year draws to a close, a remarkable initiative involving “superheroes” clad in unique costumes brings unexpected joy — not only to the sick children but also, perhaps even more deeply, to their parents.
item.dc:creator: Florent Bajrami And Llazar Semini, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Thousands of Syrians celebrate in central Damascus during first Friday prayers since Assad's fall
item.description: Thousands of Syrians celebrated in Umayyad Square, the largest in Damascus, after the first Muslim Friday prayers following the downfall of former President Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: Albert Aji And Matthew Lee, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Suspected Chinese spy with business ties to Prince Andrew barred from UK
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A suspected Chinese spy with business ties to Prince Andrew has been barred from the U.K. because of concerns he poses a threat to national security.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China's military is staying silent on its recent activity around Taiwan
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China's military kept silent Friday on its large deployment of naval and coast guard ships off Taiwan this week, with its top spokesperson quoting an ancient strategist instead.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Jersey drones: Sheriff calls for state of emergency
item.description: Shaun Golden, the sheriff of Monmouth County, New Jersey, says that more government assistance is needed to identify the drones.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Indian teen Gukesh Dommaraju becomes the youngest chess world champion after beating Chinese rival
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — Indian teenager Gukesh Dommaraju became the youngest chess world champion Thursday after beating the defending champion Ding Liren of China in the final match of their series in Singapore.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ontario to restrict electricity exports to US and bar American-made alcohol if Trump tariffs applied
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada’s most populous province may bar American-made alcohol in addition to restricting electricity exports to Michigan, New York and Minnesota if U.S. President-elect Donald Trump imposes sweeping tariffs on all Canadian products, a senior official said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: In Lesotho, UN chief Guterres urges rich nations to meet new commitments on climate finance
item.description: MASERU, Lesotho (AP) — United Nations Secretary-General António Guterres on Thursday urged rich nations to honor their commitments to help the world's poor countries fight climate change in a speech to Lesotho's Parliament and repeated his hope that Africa would soon have permanent seats on the U.N.
item.dc:creator: KEKETSO PHAKELA and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jake Sullivan says Netanyahu 'ready to do a deal' as Hamas said to concede to Israel cease-fire demands
item.description: Hamas and Israel have signaled they may be taking more serious steps to reach a cease-fire and to secure the release of the hostages held in Gaza for more than a year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Paraguay reopens its embassy in Jerusalem, handing Israel a rare diplomatic victory
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — The small South American nation of Paraguay reopened its embassy in Jerusalem on Thursday, becoming the latest of just a small handful of countries to recognize the bitterly contested city as Israel’s capital.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bolivia extradites former anti-narcotics chief to the US to face drug trafficking charges
item.description: LA PAZ, Bolivia (AP) — Bolivia's former anti-narcotics chief was extradited to the United States on Thursday to face federal drug trafficking charges in a New York court.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Concern over Syria's chemical weapons stockpile triggers emergency meeting of global monitor
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — The global chemical weapons watchdog opened an emergency meeting on Thursday to discuss the situation in Syria over concerns about the country’s stockpile of toxic chemicals in the wake of the overthrow of President Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trudeau: Harris loss a setback for women in politics
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau on Tuesday addressed Vice President Harris’s loss in the 2024 presidential election, calling it a setback for gender representation in politics, during a gala promoting women in politics.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: American freed after 7 months detention in Syria
item.description: An American citizen detained in Syria was freed Thursday after rebels overtook the city of Damascus, toppling the rule of President Bashar Assad and releasing thousands from imprisonment according to The Associated Press. Pete Travis Timmerman, 29, from Urbana, Mo.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trump has invited Xi Jinping to attend inauguration
item.description: President-elect Trump has invited Chinese President Xi Jinping to attend his inauguration next month in Washington, a spokesperson confirmed Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Florida Republican brings 'America First' bent to powerful Foreign Affairs Committee
item.description: Rep. Brian Mast’s (R-Fla.) surprising selection to be the next chair of the House Foreign Affairs Committee is the latest sign of the Republican Party being reshaped by the next generation of "America First," pro-Trump leaders.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, The Hill

item.title: Fresh fin whale meat is auctioned for the first time in decades in Japan
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Meat from fin whales caught for the first time in nearly 50 years off Japan’s northern coast fetched up to more than $1,300 per kilogram (2.2 lbs) at auction Thursday, as officials try to keep the struggling industry alive.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Coffee prices hit record high after challenging growing season
item.description: Arabica coffee sold for $3.44 a pound on the global market earlier this week, beating the prior record high of $3.35 set in 1977.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: As tourists discover Finland's Santa Claus Village, some locals call for rules to control the masses
item.description: ROVANIEMI, Finland (AP) — Shuffling across icy ground on a cold December afternoon, lots of tourist groups poured into Santa Claus Village, a winter-themed amusement park perched on the edge of the Arctic Circle.
item.dc:creator: JAMES BROOKS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US troops to remain in Syria, White House says
item.description: The ouster of Bashar al-Assad has left Biden’s outgoing administration to maneuver through a volatile moment in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Lawmakers call for intelligence assessment on ending U.S. support for Ukraine
item.description: Lawmakers want the key intelligence chiefs to assess U.S. security risks if America stops providing weapons to Ukraine and Russian wins the war. The lawmakers are pressing for a provision to be added to the National Defense Authorization Act, which the House is expected to vote on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Gabbard tests trust of allies as Trump’s potential intel director
item.description: Controversy over President-elect Trump’s pick for intelligence chief, former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), is raising uncomfortable questions for America’s closest allies over whether the U.S. will remain a trusted partner for sharing critical, sensitive information.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: US condemns Taliban banning women from medical training
item.description: The State Department on Wednesday condemned the Taliban’s newest order preventing women from studying medical courses, citing years of mistreatment.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: US Navy defeats Houthi attacks in Gulf of Aden for second time in weeks
item.description: U.S. Navy destroyers USS Stockdale (DDG 106) and USS O’Kane (DDG 77) successfully defeated a range of Houthi-launched weapons while transiting the Gulf of Aden for the second time.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu vows to challenge 'absurd' corruption charges as he takes the stand in his trial
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu promised defiantly on Tuesday to knock down corruption allegations against him as he began testifying in his long-running trial, becoming the first sitting Israeli leader to take the stand as a criminal defendant.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel bombs hundreds of sites across Syria as army pushes into border zone
item.description: Israel said Tuesday it had bombed more than 350 military sites in Syria during the previous 48 hours, targeting “most of the strategic weapons stockpiles” in the country.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Journalists' rights group says 104 media workers killed this year
item.description: More than half of the journalists and media workers killed perished during the Israel-Hamas war in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Syria's unaccounted for chemical weapons 'extremely worrying,' UN official says
item.description: Syrian dictator Bashar al-Assad was finally toppled after 13 years of civil war, having ruled Syria since 2000. The world's attention turns to Assad's unaccounted stockpile of chemical weapons.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US hostage envoy in Beirut to seek information on missing journalist Austin Tice
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. government's top hostage negotiator is in Beirut in hopes of collecting information on the whereabouts of Austin Tice, an American journalist missing in Syria for 12 years, the State Department said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Taylor Swift’s Eras Tour ends by shattering own record, grossing an estimated $2.2B, Pollstar says
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Taylor Swift’s Eras Tour brought in $2.2 billion in its nearly two-year run, making it the highest-grossing tour of all time for a second year in a row, Pollstar estimates from data collected across 149 shows.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Syrian community in Chicago celebrates end of Assad regime
item.description: It was a sea of green flags on Michigan Avenue Sunday as hundreds of Chicagoans celebrated the end of the regime in Syria.
item.dc:creator: Emmy Victor, NewsNation

item.title: Notre Dame opens to the public for first time since 2019 fire
item.description: Later on Sunday, an evening Mass will welcome members of the public who secured reservations last week.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: US will remain in eastern Syria and seek to prevent IS resurgence, Pentagon says
item.description: Hours after Syrian rebels announced they had toppled Bashar al-Assad's government, the U.S. deputy assistant secretary of defense for the Middle East said the U.S. will remain in eastern Syria to prevent an ISIS resurgence.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Syrian government falls in stunning end to 50-year rule of Assad family
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Syrian government fell early Sunday in a stunning end to the 50-year rule of the Assad family after a sudden rebel offensive sprinted across government-held territory and entered the capital in 10 days.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and ZEINA KARAM, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: South Korea's president avoids an impeachment attempt over short-lived martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea's embattled President Yoon Suk Yeol avoided an opposition-led attempt to impeach him over his short-lived imposition of martial law, as most ruling party lawmakers boycotted a parliamentary vote Saturday to deny a two-thirds majority needed to suspend his presid
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump is welcomed by Macron to Paris with presidential pomp and joined by Zelenskyy for their talks
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron welcomed Donald Trump to Paris on Saturday with a full dose of presidential pomp as the two held a hastily arranged meeting with Ukraine’s Volodymyr Zelenskyy before celebrating the grand reopening of the Notre Dame Cathedral.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: United Kingdom cracks down on junk food TV advertisements
item.description: The United Kingdom's Department of Health estimates that 7.2 billion calories will be removed from the diets of British children.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Prann, NewsNation
